rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Permission To Intervene In Lawsuit Against Purchaser Of 19th Street Property

A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: T-C 888 BRANNAN OWNER LLC, VS. NESHIAN MERIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For (Applications)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF T-C 888 BRANNAN OWNER LLC, Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right to Attach Order is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that right to attach is applicable in this case given its procedural posture. As to the alternative relief requested in the last paragraph of Makino Declaration, such relief is denied. It is not supported by authority and there appears to be no notice to the Sherriff. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1367 NATOMA ST SF, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. GJ CREATIVE EQUITY GROUP LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc., A California Demurrer To Amended Complaint (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2022 line 3. Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc., A California Demurrer To Amended Complaint (For Tentative Ruling Only) (part 2 of 4) Plaintiff's allegation that "[u]nder the TIC Agreement, [Plaintiff], Gallin, and Jones understood and agreed that Gallin and Jones would list, market, and sell the TIC Interests in their capacity as real estate agents under Vanguard's broker's license in exchange for payment of the sales commission" fails for the same reason. In fact, Vanguard is not referenced or named anywhere in the TIC Agreement. The Listing Agreement, which is neither signed by Vanguard, Gallin, or Jones, is dated almost one year after Plaintiff entered into contract with GJ Creative for its consulting and marketing services. Accordingly, Vanguard was not responsible for supervising activities of Gallin and Jones that were not activities of a real estate salesperson and that were performed on behalf of a different principal. For the same reasons, there was no basis for Plaintiff to believe that it was working with Vanguard when it entered into the TIC Agreement with GJ Creative under an ostensible agency theory of liability. Plaintiff does not dispute that it entered into the TIC Agreement with GJ Creative. The document itself is labeled "GJ Creative Equity Group TIC Consulting and Marketing Agreement." Plaintiff claims "on information and belief" that the TIC Agreement was "drafted by GJ Creative with approval of Vanguard," (SAC, 8), but does not allege what information led them to this belief. There was no contemporaneous Listing Agreement with Gallen, Jones, or Vanguard entered into at the time the TIC Agreement was executed, and there are no allegations suggesting that Gallin or Jones made any representations of authority on behalf of Vanguard or any other broker. (part 2 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: 1367 NATOMA ST SF, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. GJ CREATIVE EQUITY GROUP LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc., A California Demurrer To Amended Complaint (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2022 line 3. Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc., A California Demurrer To Amended Complaint (For Tentative Ruling Only) (part 3 of 4) 2nd Cause of Action, Rescission: Plaintiff's entire claim for rescission relies on Vanguard not being a party to the TIC Agreement. (SAC, 21.) In other words, at least according to the SAC, if Vanguard is a party to the contract, there are no grounds for rescission. If Vanguard is not a party to the contract, there are grounds to rescind the TIC Agreement with GJ Creative. Thus, Plaintiff's necessarily acknowledge that no valid claim for rescission exists against Vanguard. 3rd Cause of Action, Breach of Contract: Plaintiff failed to state a cause of action for breach of contract as against Vanguard. Gallin and Jones signed the contract on behalf of GJ Creative. Vanguard is not mentioned in the contract. None of the new allegations are sufficient to show that Plaintiff retained Gallin or Jones as real estate salespersons for services rendered under the TIC Agreement, (SAC, 4,5 8, 9,11, 12, 17), when read together with the actual contract. None of the conduct that forms the basis for the contract claims-cost of construction, hiring incompetent contractors, misrepresenting progress, failing to manage construction, failing to keep property secure, failing to ensure demolition work didn't compromise the property- is activity that requires a real estate license, and had nothing to do with the marketing and sale of the TIC Interests. 4th Cause of Action, Fraud: The SAC is devoid of the allegations required in order to successfully plead fraud as against Vanguard; namely, that Gallin and Jones had any authority to speak on behalf of Vanguard. The fact that the members of GJ Creative, i.e., Gallin and Jones, were affiliated with Vanguard at the time they provided TIC consultation services under the Contract to Plaintiff on behalf of Creative Equity is insufficient to hold Vanguard liable for any alleged representations made by Gallin and Jones. (See Hobart v. Hobart Estate Co. (1945) 26 Cal.2d 412, 449-452 [holding that there was no evidence that the defendant had authority to bind the defendant corporation to alleged misrepresentations].) (part 3 of 4) =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 1367 NATOMA ST SF, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. GJ CREATIVE EQUITY GROUP LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT (part 1 of 4) is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. 1st Cause of Action: Plaintiff fails to show that a fiduciary relationship existed between Vanguard and Plaintiff. Plaintiff fails to show that Vanguard was P's real estate broker or that an ostensible agency existed. The terms of the TIC Agreement clearly establish that Gallin and Jones, on behalf of GJ Creative, would perform non-real estate licensed activity in connection with the creation of the TIC Interests in exchange for a consulting fee. Moreover, none of the alleged wrongful conduct upon which the cause of action is predicated, (SAC 13), has anything to do with the listing, sale, leasing, exchange of or lending on real property, or any other conduct for which a real estate license is required. (Bus. & Prof. Code 10131; 10159.2.) While the TIC Agreement generally provides that Gallin and Jones were to "coordinate efforts to market and sell the TIC Interests," they were not contracted or authorized to list or sell the TIC Interests under the TIC Agreement. Plaintiff's allegations that Gallin and Jones were contracted to "solicit prospective buyers, list the TIC Interests for sale, and negotiate the purchase and sale of the TIC Interests," (SAC, 11), is not supported (if not completely undermined) by the terms of the agreement that expressly state that P was free to retain any agent or broker to market and sell the TIC interests once they were created. (Id. at Ex. B, 3(B).) (part 1 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: 1620 BROADWAY U6 LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. GJ CREATIVE EQUITY GROUP LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2022 line 4. (part 1 of 4) DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. 1st Cause of Action: Plaintiff fails to show that a fiduciary relationship existed between Vanguard and Plaintiff. Plaintiff fails to show that Vanguard was P's real estate broker or that an ostensible agency existed. The terms of the TIC Agreement clearly establish that Gallin and Jones, on behalf of GJ Creative, would perform non-real estate licensed activity in connection with the creation of the TIC Interests in exchange for a consulting fee. Moreover, none of the alleged wrongful conduct upon which the cause of action is predicated, (SAC 13), has anything to do with the listing, sale, leasing, exchange of or lending on real property, or any other conduct for which a real estate license is required. (Bus. & Prof. Code 10131; 10159.2.) While the TIC Agreement generally provides that Gallin and Jones were to "coordinate efforts to market and sell the TIC Interests," they were not contracted or authorized to list or sell the TIC Interests under the TIC Agreement. Plaintiff's allegations that Gallin and Jones were contracted to "solicit prospective buyers, list the TIC Interests for sale, and negotiate the purchase and sale of the TIC Interests," (SAC, 11), is not supported (if not completely undermined) by the terms of the agreement that expressly state that P was free to retain any agent or broker to market and sell the TIC interests once they were created. (Id. at Ex. B, 3(B).) Plaintiff's allegation that "[u]nder the TIC Agreement, [Plaintiff], Gallin, and Jones understood and agreed that Gallin and Jones would list, market, and sell the TIC Interests in their capacity as real estate agents under Vanguard's broker's license in exchange for payment of the sales commission" fails for the same reason. In fact, Vanguard is not referenced or named anywhere in the TIC Agreement. The Listing Agreement, which is neither signed by Vanguard, Gallin, or Jones, is dated almost one year after Plaintiff entered into contract with GJ Creative for its consulting and marketing services. Accordingly, Vanguard was not responsible for supervising activities of Gallin and Jones that were not activities of a real estate salesperson and that were performed on behalf of a different principal. (part 1 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: 26 THIRD STREET (SF) OWNER, LLC, A DELAWARE VS. ZAIN AZZGHAYER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 29, 2022 line 10. DEFENDANT ZAIN AZZGHAYER, DEAN AZZGHAYER, MUHAMMED ZUGHAIYIR MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of Withdrawal on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
